mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real retail sales reflects robust consumer spending, which is the primary driver of corporate revenue and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS increases coincide with negative S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 50% of months over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5%, sustained restrictive levels raise corporate borrowing costs and reduce credit availability, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 5%, S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and payroll employment signal a joint contraction in supply and labor income that reduces aggregate demand and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in INDPRO and PAYEMS fail to predict negative S&P 500 20-day returns with an information coefficient above 0.05 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment exceeds 6%, payroll employment growth signals a recovering labor market that boosts consumer confidence and spending, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days; this effect vanishes when unemployment is low.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 6%, positive PAYEMS surprises coincide with negative S&P 500 5-day returns in more than 50% of instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month drop in industrial production exceeding 1% signals manufacturing contraction and inventory destocking, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO month-over-month declines exceeding 1% coincide with positive S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices combined with stagnant or falling retail sales create a stagflationary squeeze on real household income and corporate margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive CPIAUCSL momentum and negative RSAFS growth fail to produce negative S&P 500 20-day returns with Rank IC > 0.04 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: With the federal funds rate below 1%, markets discount prolonged monetary accommodation that compresses discount rates and supports equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Periods with FEDFUNDS below 1% do not exhibit statistically significant positive S&P 500 5-day returns compared to baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 1,200 thousand units signal weakening residential investment and downstream demand for construction materials and durable goods, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST readings below 1200 coincide with positive S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 50% of months over a 3-year rolling window.
